name: Big Bend Community College
setsapartstatement: Our students are taught and trained by certified industry professionals and exit our program fully ready for employment.
screeningcriteria: Any student with a high school degree or equivalent can enroll in both programs.
recruitingsources: We recruit students from underdeveloped and rural surrounding areas such as local high schools and workforce centers.

name: Lake Washington Institute of Technology, Redmond
Lake Washington Institute of Technology, Kirkland
South Puget Sound Community College, Olympia
South Puget Sound Community College, Lacey
Career Connect SW STEM Network ESD 112
mission: Career Connect SW engages with K12, higher education, business, industry and the community to expand the skilled workforce through work-related learning opportunities that serve K12 students in every community in the ESD 112 region.
setsapartstatement: Students who complete this industry-aligned program are well-prepared to participate in the growing tech sector among the small, rural communities in the Columbia River Gorge.
screeningcriteria: Any high school student in a participating school district is eligible to participate in this training program. No previous work experience is required.
recruitingsources: All eligible students in this training program are recruited from school districts in communities considered rural and remote by the US FDA Guidelines.

name: CodeDay x MinT
mission: There's a place in tech for everyone. CodeDay is a non-profit providing welcoming, hands-on opportunities for under-served students to explore a future in tech and beyond.
setsapartstatement: Students from CodeDay Labs have already contributed to real software projects maintained by leading technology companies with 10K-10M+ users.
screeningcriteria: Students are recommended by their professors, they are juniors or seniors studying CS.
recruitingsources: Students are recruited from diverse schools, over 80% are from a demographic under-represented in the technology industry.
cost: No cost for students, $600-1200 for schools/foundations to sponsor a seat

name: North Central Educational Service District 171
setsapartstatement: Students from our courses possess hands-on experience and specialized knowledge in managing and maintain data center infrastructure, making them well-prepared for critical IT roles.
screeningcriteria: High school students select their introduction courses.  When the student chooses to continue the track they are must pass the class in-order to maintain their status of enrollment.
recruitingsources: With high school students as our primary recruits, we work to equip them with practical skills and certifications, enhancing their employability and contributing to a more skilled workforce, which drives economic growth and innovation.

name: Wenatchee Valley College
mission: Wenatchee Valley College enhances North Central Washington by meeting the educational and cultural needs of its diverse communities while promoting diversity, equity, and inclusion through high-quality educational programs.
setsapartstatement: WVC programs; Environmental Systems, Electronics, Computer Technology, and Engineering offer a unique combination of practical skills and theoretical knowledge in critical systems management, making them exceptionally equipped to enhance efficiency and reliability in IT environments, including data centers and industrial facilities.
screeningcriteria: To be eligible for the associate degree or a certificate, students must earn at least a cumulative 2.0 grade point average. Core program courses may have prerequisite requirements. English and mathematics courses require qualifying assessment scores or acceptable coursework in those subjects.
recruitingsources: Wenatchee Valley College recruits candidates primarily from underrepresented communities and local high schools, and by providing them with targeted training, we enhance workforce diversity, drive innovation, and stimulate economic growth in the region.

name: Per Scholas Seattle
mission: Per Scholas’ mission is to advance economic equity through rigorous training for tech careers and to connect skilled talent to leading businesses.
providerdescription: Per Scholas provides tuition-free tech training, expanding access to in-demand careers and creating pathways to economic mobility. We equip individuals with the skills needed for high-growth jobs and partner with employers to build sustainable talent pipelines that drive business success.
setsapartstatement: Our programs bridge the gap between untapped talent and businesses needing skilled workers, fueling personal and economic growth. By fostering a workforce that reflects America’s population across urban, suburban, and rural communities, we empower individuals to unlock their potential and strengthen their communities, families, and businesses.
screeningcriteria:
"Eligibility Requirements:
18 years or older with a passion for technology
High School diploma or equivalent
Able to provide proof of U.S. Work Authorization
Committed to starting a full-time job after course completion
Reside within eligible distance from campus
Pass all required Enrollment steps, such as assessments, pre-work and documentation
Meet learner pre-training income policy criteria
Can read, write, and speak in English proficiently
Able to commit to the instructor-led time commitment required for the course, typically, Mon-Fri 9 AM - 4 PM for Full-Time courses
"
recruitingsources: "We serve as a tech educational and training partner for an expansive group of DEI-focused community-based organizations (ie. Refugee Washington Alliance, Asian Counseling and Referral Service, Urban League of Metro Seattle, and others) that drive our goal targets and results of serving 85% people of color and 40% women as well as connecting them with lucrative entry to mid-level tech jobs that result in a 3 to 4x increase in their incomes allowing them to earn sustainable, livable wages which in turn gets reinvested in the communities in which they live.

name: PRCCDC/PNW Cyber Challenge Games
mission: PNW Cyber Challenge Games runs cyber competitions to expose students to  real world cyber-attacks.
setsapartstatement: Studies show that students who participate in cyber competitions develop technical and soft skills that better prepare them for the workplace by providing real-world experience.
screeningcriteria: Students have to be registered full time at a school that is sending a team to the event. The schools can be two-year, four-year college or university.  No work experience is required.
recruitingsources: The goal of PNW Cyber Challenge Games is to attract more women, students of color and disadvantaged students to the profession of being a cyber defender.

name: Riipen
mission: Riipen is a work-based learning platform helping educators, organizations, and learners collaborate on real industry projects to bridge the gap between education and employment.
providerdescription: Deliver online employer-designed projects for students via its online work-based learning marketplace providing a highly effective, high-quality, low-cost, inclusive and accessible mechanism for providing skills training and aligning training with employer workforce needs.
setsapartstatement: Unlock 40-hour, fully-subsidized, digital tech internships, specialized in web/app development, cloud computing and data analytics for your Washington business, building and nurturing your tech talent pipeline.
screeningcriteria: All participants are recent graduates or current students of North Seattle College's Application Development BAS and Computer Science BS.
recruitingsources: The Tech Talent Boost Washington (TTBW) recruits candidates from North Seattle College, which typically serves a diverse student body including underrepresented groups, career changers, and working adults. These students come from a variety of backgrounds, building hands-on skills in fields like application development and computer science. By training these students through employer-led projects, businesses not only close the skills gap but also fuel economic growth by developing a well-prepared workforce that drives innovation in the tech sector.

name: SkillSource
mission: SkillSource provides training and learning opportunities in North Central Washington to help residents of all ages build new careers and help businesses develop strong teams.
setsapartstatement: They are fully trained to meet job requirements as defined by industry professionals.
screeningcriteria: Individuals must be eligible to work in the United States and meet other eligibility requirements for federal and state workforce development programs. Businesses must be licensed to operate in Washington State.
recruitingsources: SkillSource specifically seeks to support individuals with barriers to employment and completion of traditional training pathways, including economically disadvantaged individuals, persons of color, individuals with disabilities, and underserved populations, in order to improve their career and economic outcomes and build stronger communities.
cost: On-The-Job Training reimburses up to 75% of employee wages to the employer. Upskilling training requires a 10% to 50% employer match (may be met by employee wages) and reimburses up to 100% of the cost of training. Registered apprenticeship is at no cost to the individual (and in fact the student is a hired and paid employee of the Wenatchee School District).

name: Year Up Puget Sound
mission: Year Up United's mission is to close the Opportunity Divide by ensuring young adults gain the skills, experiences, and support to empower them to reach their potential through careers and higher education.
setsapartstatement: Year Up United graduates are equipped with not only technical and industry-specific skills but also the essential soft skills, such as communication and problem-solving, that make them immediately effective in the workplace.
screeningcriteria: Candidates for Year Up United must be between 18 to 29 years old, possess a high school diploma or GED, and demonstrate financial need. No prior work experience is required. Year Up United’s program model is designed to serve Opportunity Talent, and therefore, we admit students who demonstrate the most need and readiness for the education, training, and support that Year Up United provides.
recruitingsources: Year Up United recruits young adults from low-income and underrepresented communities, and by providing them with professional skills and job placements, we are closing the Opportunity Divide, driving upward economic mobility, and creating a more inclusive workforce.

name: Minneapolis College of Art and Design
Community College of the Air Force
The West African Union University Benin Republic
The University of Texas at Austin
SP Jain Institute of Management & Research
College of Business Studies, University of Delhi
Kabul Polytechnic University Faculty of Computer Engineering & Informatics
Washington Vets to Tech (Saint Martin University)
mission: WaV2T's mission is '"To provide computer science and information technology education, training, and professional development to active-duty service members in their last 6 months of service, veterans, and military spouses.",
providerdescription: WaV2T graduates offer a unique combination of military discipline, entry-level to advanced IT expertise across multiple domains, and strong problem-solving abilities, all supported by comprehensive in-person instruction, making them well-equipped to meet the evolving demands of today’s tech industry.
setsapartstatement: WaV2T graduates offer a unique combination of military discipline, entry-level to advanced IT expertise across multiple domains, and strong problem-solving abilities, all supported by comprehensive in-person instruction, making them well-equipped to meet the evolving demands of today’s tech industry.
screeningcriteria: Must be an active duty service member in the US military within 180 days of their end of service date, veterans with an honorable or general under honorable conditions discharge, or a military spouse.
recruitingsources: Our primary pool of applicants are from active duty military at Joint Base Lewis-McChord (leaving the military within 180 days) and local veterans, providing them with critical IT skills that not only enhance their post-service career prospects but also strengthen the local economy by filling high-demand roles in the tech industry.
cost: Most program costs are covered for students via VA benefits. Students without VA benefits or who demonstrate financial need by be eligible for financial assistance.

name: University of Washington Professional and Continuing Education
Washington State Controls Specialist Apprenticeship Committee - Associate Controls Specialist
Washington State Controls Specialist Apprenticeship Committee - Controls Programmer
Liceo Concejo De Bello High School
MIT Professional Education – Emerging Talent Program

College of Business Studies, University of Delhi, India
Goodwill of the Olympics and Rainier Region
